content.problemStatement:
Let us define a special array as an array of **distinct natural** numbers such that the sum of any three elements is **not** divisible by 555.

Formally, let AAA be an array of size NNN. Then, AAA is a special array if the following conditions hold:


Ai‚ààN‚ÄÖ‚Ää‚ÄÖ‚Ää‚ÄÖ‚Ää‚ÄÖ‚Ää‚àÄ‚ÄÖ1‚â§i‚â§NA_i \in \mathbb{N} \;\;\;\; \forall \: 1 \leq i \leq NAi‚Äã‚ààN‚àÄ1‚â§i‚â§N;
Ai‚â†Aj‚ÄÖ‚Ää‚ÄÖ‚Ää‚ÄÖ‚Ää‚àÄ‚ÄÖ‚Ää1‚â§i‚â†j‚â§N A_i \neq A_j \;\;\; \forall \; 1 \leq i \neq j \leq NAi‚ÄãÓÄ†=Aj‚Äã‚àÄ1‚â§iÓÄ†=j‚â§N;
(Ai+Aj+Ak)(A_i + A_j + A_k)(Ai‚Äã+Aj‚Äã+Ak‚Äã) %\%% 5‚â†0‚ÄÖ‚Ää‚ÄÖ‚Ää‚ÄÖ‚Ää‚àÄ‚ÄÖ(i,j,k)5 \neq 0 \;\;\; \forall \: (i, j, k)5ÓÄ†=0‚àÄ(i,j,k) where  1‚â§i<j<k‚â§N1 \leq i < j < k \leq N1‚â§i<j<k‚â§N.
Let us define the score of the special array as the **sum** of all its elements.

Sarthak has an array AAA comprising NNN elements, but unfortunately, he has forgotten the values at certain indices within the array. His aim is to reconstruct the array AAA such that AAA becomes a special array.

Out of all the possible arrays AAA that meet this criterion, he wants to know the one with the **minimum score**. What is the minimum attainable score?


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case consists of a single integer NNN, the size of the special array.
The second line contains NNN spaced integers: A1,A2,....ANA_1, A_2, .... A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,....AN‚Äã, where Ai‚ààNA_i \in \mathbb{N}Ai‚Äã‚ààN means Sarthak remembers it whereas Ai=‚àí1A_i = -1Ai‚Äã=‚àí1 means that AiA_iAi‚Äã is missing and has to be recovered.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the minimum possible sum of elements of a special array which can be recovered from AAA or print ‚àí1-1‚àí1, if no such array is possible.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all testcases does not exceed 2‚àó1052*10^52‚àó105

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: One optimal array is [11,16,5,1,10,6,2][11, 16, 5, 1, 10, 6, 2][11,16,5,1,10,6,2].
The sum of this array is 515151.
Test case 222: It can be shown that no matter how the reconstruction is done, the resulting array won't be special.
